title: Tapiphyllum psammophilum
taxonomy: Tapiphyllum psammophilum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a member of the genus Tapiphyllum, which consists of several species of shrubs and trees. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Australia and is commonly found in sandy soils. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The species is considered rare and is often studied by botanists for its unique characteristics.

It is said to be used to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and skin infections.
